8.text:
said to be the "triple digit" number because they are the product of five primes. The remaining digits are the "decimal" number because they are the product of three primes.

The reason for this is that the number of possible iterations of pi is finite. The number of possible values of any given number includes two and three.

The digits of pi are the product of five primes.

The digits of pi are the product of three primes.
